zh.lifeSummary: 當Alexander de Néville於大約1210年出生在英國達勒姆時，他的父親Alexander de Néville I24歲，母親Margaret de Redbourne32歲。他於1227年與Julliana de Monmouth在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1249年7月12日在英國林可爾史爾雷德布恩享年40歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Néville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Neuville in Calvados or Néville in Seine-Maritime, both so called from Old French neu(f) ‘new’ (from Latin novus) + ville ‘settlement’. One family line, originally from Neuville (Calvados), became powerful during the Wars of the Roses through Richard Neville (1428–71), 16th Earl of Warwick, nicknamed "The Kingmaker".
Irish (Munster): assimilation of the Gaelic name Ó Niadh (see Nee ) and sometimes of Ó Cnaimhín (see Nevin ).

History: George Neville came to VA in or c. 1700 and settled on the headwaters of the Occoquan River, acquiring a large estate. His descendants, bearing the surnames Neville and Craig, were of considerable importance in Pittsburgh, PA, and Cincinnati, OH.

variants.name: Nevells
meanings: Irish: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevils
meanings: English: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Novel
meanings: English: variant of   Neville  .
Catalan: variant of   Novell  .

variants.name: Newell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .
English: variant of   Noel  .
English: variant of   Newhall  .

variants.name: Nevills
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Nevil
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Newill
meanings: English and Irish (or Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevill
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevels
meanings: English and Scottish (Wigtownshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Dutch and North German: unexplained.

variants.name: Nee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, John Patrick.

Irish (Galway): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Niadh ‘descendant of Nia’ or Mac Niadh ‘son of Nia’. Compare McNee . Needham 2 and Neville have also been adopted as Anglicized forms of this name.
North German: possibly a relationship name from Middle Low German neve ‘nephew, grand-child’.

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Alexander de Néville was born about 1210, in Durham, England, his father, Alexander de Néville I, was 24 and his mother, Margaret de Redbourne, was 32. He married Julliana de Monmouth in 1227, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 5 daughters. He died on 12 July 1249, in Redbourne, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alexander de Néville est né environ 1210, à Durham, Angleterre, son père, Alexander de Néville I, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Redbourne, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Julliana de Monmouth en 1227, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 12 juillet 1249, à Redbourne, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Alexander de Néville(이)가 대략 1210년 영국 뒤람에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alexander de Néville I(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Redbourne(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1227년 영국 요르크실르에서 Julliana de Monmouth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1249년 7월 12일 영국 링코실르 레드보우른에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.
